scenario_results.reason: Normal agricultural reporting package stayed tied to acreage, field-log, harvest, traceability, environmental, program, and approval records.
Ambiguous acreage, yield, production, and sustainability metric definitions forced metric-definition escalation instead of confident publication.
Conflicting farm management, FSA, scale-ticket, inventory, traceability, and dashboard records produced a reconciliation conflict note with source lineage.
Missing field logs, lot IDs, water-test records, livestock inventory, environmental period, timestamps, and retrieval hooks blocked reliable analysis.
Unsupported causal and disclosure claims were refused and routed to agronomic, environmental compliance, legal, or disclosure-review owners.
Incomplete and conflicting submission inputs produced a submission-readiness blocker instead of a program-ready assertion.
Seasonal and production-cycle dependencies were handled with interim caveats and final-report blockers until harvest and lot reconciliation close.
Food-safety and environmental risks triggered escalation, traceability scope preservation, and refusal of an unsupported clean statement.
Agronomic, veterinary, and environmental signoff requests were refused or routed with a bounded evidence package.
Crop-insurance adjustment, organic certification, grant approval, legal, environmental permit, and final submission authority were routed to adjacent owners.
Missing tenant facts, systems, record locations, source precedence, and approval owners resulted in a low-confidence blocked posture.
Conflicting tenant, buyer, state, FDA, and retrieved-record sources triggered escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific field groupings, crop-stage definitions, thresholds, certifier findings, cadence, and gates were layered over the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against a standard 0.90 threshold for agricultural reporting package assembly, metric definition, source tie-out, seasonal dependency handling, and authority boundaries.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and agriculture-and-natural-resources abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
